title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Bertemu Dengannya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_en: When the Prophet saw him, he found him to be an old man whose body had weakened and bones had softened and who was close to death. Abu Bakr looked at his father, who he now saw after a long period of separation, for he had been apart from his father in the service of his religion. The Prophet said to Abu Bakr out of kindness, "Why didn't you leave your old father at home so I could come to see him instead?" Abu Bakr knew that they were in the middle of a war where the Messenger of Allah was their leader. He also knew that the Prophet had very little time and too much to accomplish to visit the old man's house and ask him to accept Islam. Hence, Abu Bakr thanked the Prophet and said, "O Messenger of Al23 Taking one's psychological condition into consideration lah, it is more appropriate that he comes to you than for you to go to him." The Prophet sat Abu Quhafah down in front of him very kindly, placed his hand on his chest and said, "Accept Islam." Abu Quhafah's face illuminated, and he said, "I testify that there is no one worthy of worship but Allah and that Muhammad is His servant and messenger", and Abu Bakr rejoiced as he had never done before. The Prophet gazed at the old man's face and noticed that his hair had turned grey, so he said, "Dye his hair, but do not use black." Yes, he would take psychology into consideration when dealing with others. In fact, when he entered Makkah, he had divided the army into battalions and had given the banner of one of the battalions to the heroic Companion Sa'd bin 'Ubadah – may Allah be pleased with him. It was considered very noble to carry the banner, not just for the carrier but also for his people. As Sa'd was looking at Makkah and its residents, he reﬂected upon how they had fought against the Messenger of Allah , put him under unbearable strain, turned people away from him, murdered Sumayyah and Yasir, and tortured Bilal and Khabbab. He thought that they surely deserved to be taught a lesson! Sa'd waved his banner and said, "Today is the day of slaughter! Today your inviolabilities will be attacked!" This was unbearable for Quraysh. They feared that he might exterminate them all. Hence, a woman stepped forward and complained to the Prophet about Sa'd in words of poetry, begging him to prevent Sa'd from shedding anyone's blood. When Allah's Messenger heard her poetic words, he was Taking one's psychological condition into consideration moved and felt pity. He didn't want to disappoint her, just as he didn't want to anger Sa'd by taking away the banner from him after having honoured him therewith. He therefore ordered Sa'd to hand over the banner to his son, Qays bin Sa'd, whom Sa'd was riding alongside, as they entered Makkah. Hence, the woman from Quraysh was happy when she saw that Sa'd was no longer carrying the banner, and likewise, Sa'd was not angered since he remained the leader of the battalion, except that he had spared himself the burden of carrying the banner and had thus given it to his son to carry instead. How wonderful it is to kill two birds with one stone! Try not to lose anyone. Try to successfully win over everyone - even if there is conﬂict of interest between them. Harmony... We deal with hearts not bodies. Taking one's psychological condition into consideration (24) Be concerned about others People usually like to be valued. This is why one sees individuals at times acting in a certain manner in order to attract attention. They may even invent tales or stories of their valour so that people may show them concern or be amazed by them. Imagine a person who returns home from work tired. He enters his living room and ﬁnds his four children sitting alone. The oldest of them is eleven-years old and is watching a television program. The second is having his dinner. The third is playing with his toys, and the fourth is doing his homework. The father then greets them in a loud voice: "as-Salaamu 'alaykum!" One of the children is lost in his television program, the second is completely enchanted by his toys and the third is busy with his dinner. As for the fourth, when he turns around and sees his father, he drops his books and rushes in delight to welcome him, kisses his hand, and then returns to his books. Which of the four children would be the most beloved to the father? I say with certainty that our response would be the same: the most beloved of them would of course be the fourth; not because he is the most beautiful or the most intelligent, but only because he showed his father that he valued him.
content_id: Ketika Nabi melihatnya, beliau mendapatinya sebagai seorang lelaki tua yang tubuhnya telah melemah, tulang-tulangnya telah melunak, dan ia sudah dekat dengan kematian. Abu Bakr memandang ayahnya, yang kini ia lihat setelah jangka waktu perpisahan yang lama, karena ia telah terpisah dari ayahnya dalam pengabdian kepada agamanya. Nabi berkata kepada Abu Bakr dengan penuh kasih, "Mengapa kamu tidak membiarkan ayahmu yang sudah tua di rumah sehingga aku bisa datang menemuinya?" Abu Bakr tahu bahwa mereka sedang berada di tengah perang di mana Rasulullah adalah pemimpin mereka. Ia juga tahu bahwa Nabi memiliki sedikit waktu dan terlalu banyak yang harus dilakukan untuk mengunjungi rumah orang tua itu dan mengajaknya masuk Islam. Oleh karena itu, Abu Bakr berterima kasih kepada Nabi dan berkata, "Wahai Rasulullah, lebih tepat baginya untuk datang kepadamu daripada engkau datang kepadanya." Nabi mendudukkan Abu Quhafah di hadapannya dengan penuh kasih, meletakkan tangannya di dadanya dan berkata, "Masuk Islamlah." Wajah Abu Quhafah bersinar, dan ia berkata, "Aku bersaksi bahwa tidak ada yang berhak disembah selain Allah dan bahwa Muhammad adalah hamba dan utusan-Nya", dan Abu Bakr pun bersukacita seperti yang belum pernah ia rasakan sebelumnya. Nabi menatap wajah orang tua itu dan memperhatikan bahwa rambutnya telah memutih, lalu beliau bersabda, "Warnai rambutnya, tetapi jangan gunakan warna hitam." Ya, beliau selalu mempertimbangkan kondisi psikologis dalam berurusan dengan orang lain. Bahkan, ketika beliau memasuki Makkah, beliau telah membagi pasukan menjadi batalion-batalion dan memberikan panji salah satu batalion kepada sahabat yang heroik, Sa'd bin Ubadah radhiyallahu anhu. Membawa panji dianggap sangat mulia, bukan hanya bagi pembawanya tetapi juga bagi kaumnya. Saat Sa'd memandang Makkah dan penduduknya, ia merenungkan bagaimana mereka telah berperang melawan Rasulullah, menyiksanya dengan penderitaan yang tak tertahankan, memalingkan orang-orang darinya, membunuh Sumayyah dan Yasir, serta menyiksa Bilal dan Khabbab. Ia pikir mereka memang layak mendapat pelajaran! Sa'd mengibas-ngibaskan panjinya dan berkata, "Hari ini adalah hari pembantaian! Hari ini kehormatan-kehormatan kalian akan diserang!" Ini tidak tertahankan bagi Quraisy. Mereka khawatir bahwa ia mungkin akan membinasakan mereka semua. Maka, seorang wanita maju dan mengadu kepada Nabi tentang Sa'd dengan kata-kata puisi, memohon beliau untuk mencegah Sa'd dari menumpahkan darah siapa pun. Ketika Rasulullah mendengar kata-kata puisinya, beliau tergerak dan merasa iba. Beliau tidak ingin mengecewakan wanita itu, sebagaimana beliau tidak ingin membuat Sa'd marah dengan mengambil panji darinya setelah menghormatinya dengan panji tersebut. Maka beliau memerintahkan Sa'd untuk menyerahkan panji kepada putranya, Qays bin Sa'd, yang sedang menunggangi kuda di samping Sa'd, saat mereka memasuki Makkah. Dengan demikian, wanita dari Quraisy itu merasa senang ketika melihat bahwa Sa'd tidak lagi membawa panji, dan begitu pula Sa'd tidak merasa marah karena ia tetap menjadi pemimpin batalion, kecuali bahwa ia telah membebaskan dirinya dari beban membawa panji dan memberikannya kepada putranya untuk dibawa. Betapa indahnya mendapatkan dua keuntungan sekaligus! Cobalah untuk tidak kehilangan siapa pun. Cobalah untuk berhasil mendapatkan kepercayaan semua orang—meskipun ada konflik kepentingan di antara mereka. Keselarasan... Kita berurusan dengan hati, bukan tubuh. (24) Pedulilah terhadap orang lain. Orang-orang biasanya suka dihargai. Itulah mengapa seseorang kadang-kadang bertindak dengan cara tertentu untuk menarik perhatian. Mereka bahkan mungkin mengarang kisah-kisah atau cerita tentang keberanian mereka agar orang-orang menunjukkan kepedulian kepada mereka atau merasa kagum. Bayangkan seseorang yang pulang dari bekerja dalam keadaan lelah. Ia memasuki ruang tamunya dan mendapati keempat anaknya duduk sendirian. Yang tertua berusia sebelas tahun dan sedang menonton program televisi. Yang kedua sedang makan malam. Yang ketiga sedang bermain dengan mainannya, dan yang keempat sedang mengerjakan pekerjaan rumah. Sang ayah kemudian menyapa mereka dengan suara lantang: "Assalamu alaikum!" Salah seorang anak larut dalam program televisinya, yang kedua sepenuhnya terpesona oleh mainannya, dan yang ketiga sibuk dengan makan malamnya. Adapun yang keempat, ketika ia berbalik dan melihat ayahnya, ia melepaskan buku-bukunya dan berlari dengan gembira untuk menyambutnya, mencium tangannya, lalu kembali ke buku-bukunya. Anak mana di antara keempat anak itu yang akan paling dicintai oleh sang ayah? Aku katakan dengan yakin bahwa jawaban kita akan sama: yang paling dicintai tentu saja adalah yang keempat; bukan karena ia paling tampan atau paling cerdas, tetapi hanya karena ia menunjukkan kepada ayahnya bahwa ia menghargainya.
